rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical fluorosulfonylation of styrenes with sodium arylsulfinates and Et3N·3HF to give β-fluorosulfones via radical-polar crossover
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of sodium arylsulfinate (2a) to a sulfonyl radical; subsequent anodic oxidation of the benzylic radical adduct to a benzylic cation; cathodic reduction of protons generates H2 (obviating chemical oxidant)

key_intermediates.name: benzylic radical from sulfonyl addition to styrene (II)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes ArSO2Na (2a) to sulfonyl radical I, which adds to the styrene to give benzylic radical II; a second anodic oxidation forms benzylic cation III, which is trapped by fluoride from Et3N·3HF to deliver the β-fluorosulfone 3. Protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.
notes: Mechanism is proposed primarily on the basis of CV showing low oxidation potential of sulfinate 2a (oxidized first) and analogy to literature precedents; no radical-trap (TEMPO/BHT), KIE, or DFT experiments are reported on this page. The same paradigm is extended to fluorothiocyanation (with NH4SCN) and to vinylsulfonylation (with stainless-steel cathode).
